name: Bambu TPU 95A HF @P1S 0.4 (gripper fingers)
filament_start_gcode: ; Bambu TPU 95A HF (gripper Fin Ray fingers). EXTERNAL SPOOL — NOT the AMS (Bambu rates 95A HF too soft for the AMS). PLATE: textured PEI, NO glue (glue over-adheres TPU on textured PEI); bed 30-35 C. Dry 70 C 8 h first. 0.4 mm hardened nozzle.
filament_notes:
Gripper Fin Ray fingers, printed in Bambu TPU 95A HF. This preset INHERITS the Bambu system 'Bambu TPU 95A HF @BBL P1S', so the tested values come straight from Bambu: nozzle 230 C, flow ratio 1.0, max volumetric speed 12 mm3/s (HF = ~3x standard-TPU throughput), retraction 0.8 mm @ 10 mm/s, density 1.22. Only the gripper-specific reminders are overridden here.
SPOOL: external spool, NOT the AMS — Bambu's own profile states 95A HF is 'too soft and not compatible with the AMS'. Feed straight to the toolhead.
PLATE: Textured PEI, NO glue. Bambu Wiki: the textured PEI keys TPU mechanically; applying glue causes EXCESSIVE adhesion. Keep bed 30-35 C (low) so the large flat 28x96 finger footprint stays removable; release by cooling, lifting a corner, squirting IPA into the gap, then peeling slowly (don't pry a hot part - chips the PEI). A SMOOTH plate (Cool/Engineering/High-Temp) would instead NEED glue as a release barrier.
DRYING: 70 C 8 h (oven) or X1 bed 80-90 C 12 h; store <20% RH (HF is humidity-sensitive).
NOZZLE: 0.4 mm hardened/abrasion-tolerant. Pair with the 'Gripper finger TPU 0.16mm @P1S 0.4' process profile.
